free_text: Measurement of excitation functions and mean projected
recoil ranges of nuclei in alpha-induced reactions on
F,Al,V,Co and Re nuclei.
The hybrid model of Blann.
114 cm3 Ge(Li) detector
The total alpha-particle beam was collected and
measured using a calibrated ORTEC current integrator.
Additionally total beam current was also monitored
through the production of 22Na and 24Na in target
backings and catchers
Total error excluding the uncertainties
of the nuclear data used in the analysis.
The uncertainty due to target foil thickness.
The uncertainty in the beam current integration
The uncertainty in the detector efficiency.
The uncertainty caused by the large size of the
irradiation area and the non-uniformities of the target
contribute about 5% to the average error of the cr.sect
Statistical uncertainty
By CAJAD.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections in REACTION code in Subents 007-009,012,018
BIB update in all Subents. MONITOR was added. RAD-DET
deleted from Subents (info presented in DECAY-DATA).
SD: Institute code was corrected.
SD: Corrections in Subents 004-006,020,021.
The backings of gallium and antimony targets was used.
- target
description
fig. 2b from PRM,40,227,1993
SD: Data re-digitized.
The backings of gallium and antimony targets were used.
- target
description
fig. 2a from PRM,40,227,1993
SD: Data re-digitized.
The evaporated CaF(2) on Al backings were used. A thin
4 x 34 mu-m aluminum foils were kept over the target
for the protection of the material. In the fluorine
target stack there were about 12 to 15 targets.
Tbl.3 from Pramana,40(1993)227
SD: SF5=IND deleted from REACTION code.
SD: SF3=X -> N in REACTION code.
Self-supporting foils,50 mu-m thick, were used.
A 23.4 mu-m thick aluminum catcher was interspersed
between the two target foils. For vanadium stack about
ten target-catcher combination were used.
